element_introduction_order.mastery_context: player given free land and starting funds to begin construction
visitors arrive/depart via bus; entry price is player-controlled variable
low complexity attractions available from start; foundation for revenue
customizable attributes (e.g., sugar content) affect repeat customer behavior
insufficient staffing triggers footpath mess, ride breakdowns, crime, visitor unhappiness
neglected rides explode; requires mechanic staff allocation
unlocks new rides, shops, facilities; player allocates funding; improves ride durability and staff efficiency

element_introduction_order.element: Complex rides (roller coaster, Ferris wheel, track layout)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: expensive, require custom track building; generate higher revenue but more management overhead
thematic attractions available after research; platform-dependent (some versions lack specific shows)
enhance park aesthetics and visitor satisfaction; quality-of-life management
player can take loans to accelerate early expansion; financial risk management
annual judgment against rivals; drives progression pacing

element_introduction_order.first_appears: mid game (when visitor unhappiness threshold reached)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: unhappy visitors trigger thugs who pop balloons, steal food, beat entertainers; requires security staff
failure triggers staff strikes or shipment loss; dynamic economic pressure
sell park, move to new worldwide locations with different economies, weather, terrain factors

element_introduction_order.element: Worldwide park locations (desert, glacier, varied terrain)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: second/subsequent parks feature different difficulty modifiers and visual settings

element_introduction_order.first_appears: available in higher difficulty modes (not sandbox)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: complexity gates for players seeking deeper economic simulation

typical_relief_triggers: successful ride construction generating visitor revenue
visitor happiness improving after quality upgrades
typical_tension_triggers: insufficient staff causing path deterioration and ride breakdowns
ride maintenance neglect leading to explosions
limited starting capital vs. expansion needs
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Calendar-based annual cycle creates structured tension-relief rhythm. Player manages real-time operational failures (maintenance, staffing, vandalism) punctuated by yearly judgment events. Game speed is player-adjustable, allowing tension modulation. Sandbox mode removes economic pressure; higher difficulties intensify it via negotiation and logistics complexity.

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Research availability gates unlock new ride types and facilities progressively; player can explore deeper management complexity by switching to higher difficulty modes

difficulty_curve.early_game: Tutorial-like phase with basic rides (bouncy castle, tree house), simple shops, and initial visitor influx. Player learns ticket pricing, staff hiring, and basic revenue loops. Limited initial capital encourages careful placement.
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Introduction of complex rides (roller coasters requiring track layout), research system, staff management depth, and thug vandalism triggered by visitor unhappiness. Maintenance becomes critical. Loan repayment pressure increases. Negotiation mechanics appear.
difficulty_curve.late_game: Park auction milestone achieved after reaching profitability. Relocation to worldwide locations with modifiers (economy, weather, terrain) increases difficulty. Advanced players engage with stock/share/logistics systems on higher difficulties. Rivals become more challenging.
notable_spikes: First complex ride construction (roller coaster) requiring capital and track planning
Thug vandalism onset when visitor happiness drops
Annual rival judgment creating time-pressure tension
Park auction transition requiring capital accumulation milestone

notable_level_patterns.description: Game time progresses as calendar. Year-end triggers rival performance judgment and cash awards. Encourages medium-term planning (research prioritization, ride investment timing) rather than purely reactive management.

Three difficulty tiers. Sandbox removes research/negotiation/stock complexity. Higher difficulties gate advanced systems. Player can switch difficulty mid-game, allowing graduated complexity engagement.
New rides, shops, and facilities locked behind research. Player allocates research budget. Creates pacing gates preventing early access to high-revenue attractions (Ferris wheel, roller coaster).
Insufficient staffing triggers cascading failures: messy paths → visitor unhappiness → thug vandalism → further revenue loss. Incentivizes proactive hiring and maintenance allocation.
Rides degrade and explode if unmaintained. Creates invisible time pressure requiring mechanic allocation and ongoing financial commitment beyond initial construction cost.
